Project_Description: Menopausal hot flashes are sudden feelings of intense heat, elevated heart rate, and sweating that last 1-5 minutes and may occur multiple times a day. Affecting 85% of women, these symptoms persist for an average of 11 years. CoolFlash aims to mitigate hot flash symptoms by delivering automatic, targeted cooling to the user across a broad surface area of the body.

awards.title: Esch Pitch Competition Finalists
awards.details: Awarded $12,000 for extreme entrepreneurial skills in the pursuit of research and development and its commercialization for the benefit of Canada

awards.title: CABHI Next Gen Program
awards.details: Awarded $2,500 and support to accelerate innovation to impact, as part of the Centre for Aging + Brain Health Innovation Program.

awards.title: Engineers of the Future Award
awards.details: Awarded $200 for demonstrating exceptional engineering skills and innovation in a capstone project.

Team_Members: Kate Avison, Jordyn Maywood, Maya DeVries, Mariam Busari
Favourite_Memory: The first time our prototyped worked and we had our "this is all coming together" moment
Biggest_Challenges: Obtaining the materials needed in small quantities
Takeaway: Understanding your user group and finding a true problem can make the whole experience so much more rewarding
Advice: If you find a problem you are truly passionate about, spending hours working on the device will be so much easier
Whats_Next: We are strongly considering the velocity accelerate program and working on making the first protoype over the summer
Contact_Information: Kate Avison (kavison@uwaterloo.ca), Jordyn Maywood (jgmaywood@uwaterloo.ca), Maya DeVries (m5devrie@uwaterloo.ca), Mariam Busari (mbusari@uwaterloo.ca)
